title: oryzopsis exigua var. hendersonii
intro: Oryzopsis exigua var. hendersonii, also known as Henderson's Ricegrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant is native to the western parts of North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and as an expectorant to help relieve coughs and congestion. The health benefits of Oryzopsis exigua var. hendersonii include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, and it can also be used in tincture or extract form to harness its medicinal properties.
